description: Trigger accuracy evals for the monte-carlo-compare-trace skill. Each case specifies whether the skill SHOULD or SHOULD NOT be triggered by the given prompt.

cases.prompt: Compare these two agent conversations: 019e8f2a-24ae-7880-8901-cbc79aca43ed and 019e9319-e88e-7080-bc78-2aff46543849
cases.rationale: Direct A/B compare with two conversation IDs -- core skill use case

cases.prompt: Diff these two agent runs and tell me what changed.
cases.rationale: Explicit 'diff two agent runs' phrasing -- listed trigger

cases.prompt: I tweaked the system prompt for the coverage agent and re-ran it on the same conversation. Did the change cause a regression? Here are the two IDs.
cases.rationale: Prompt change regression check between two runs -- explicit use case
Explicit slash-command invocation -- skill's own command

cases.prompt: Here are two conversation IDs from the chat agent — show me how the tool sequences differ.
cases.rationale: Tool-sequence diff between two conversations -- tool_call_diff evaluator territory

cases.prompt: We swapped the agent model from claude-3-5 to claude-sonnet-4 and re-ran a fixed scenario. Compare the two runs.
cases.rationale: Model-swap A/B with two runs to compare -- explicit use case

cases.prompt: Compare the OTel traces from these two agent runs and produce a side-by-side report.
cases.rationale: Trace-level comparison with HTML-report intent -- matches skill output

cases.prompt: I have two agent traces I want to look at side-by-side. Conversation IDs are X and Y.
cases.rationale: Side-by-side trace comparison with both IDs supplied

cases.prompt: Show me the difference in graph path between baseline and candidate runs of the coverage agent.
cases.rationale: Graph-path diff between two runs -- graph_path_diff evaluator territory

cases.prompt: Did removing the get_use_cases tool change how the coverage agent handles 'what's my coverage gap?' Compare a before and after run.
cases.rationale: Tool-loadout change A/B between two runs -- explicit use case

cases.prompt: Compare the latency and token usage between these two agent conversations.
cases.rationale: Latency/token diff between two runs -- latency_diff evaluator territory

cases.prompt: What went wrong with this agent run? Here's the conversation ID.
cases.rationale: Single-trace troubleshooting -- not a comparison; routes to analyze-root-cause / incident-response

cases.prompt: Investigate why this trace failed. The conversation ID is 019e8f2a-24ae-7880-8901-cbc79aca43ed.
cases.rationale: Single-trace failure investigation -- not an A/B comparison

cases.prompt: Compare row counts between our staging and production orders tables.
cases.rationale: Cross-table data comparison -- routes to monitoring-advisor (comparison monitor); not agent A/B

cases.prompt: How does my chat agent perform overall? Show me aggregate metrics.
cases.rationale: Aggregate performance question with no two specific conversation IDs to compare

cases.prompt: Set up an evaluation monitor for my chat agent to track response quality over time.
cases.rationale: Agent eval monitor creation -- routes to monitoring-advisor

cases.prompt: Diff these two SQL queries and tell me which one is more efficient.
cases.rationale: SQL comparison -- wrong domain (not agent traces)

cases.prompt: Show me the trace for conversation 019e8f2a-24ae-7880-8901-cbc79aca43ed.
cases.rationale: Single-trace inspection -- not a comparison

cases.prompt: Help me build a prompt eval framework for my LangGraph agent.
cases.rationale: Generic eval-framework engineering -- not an A/B compare on existing runs

cases.prompt: Compare these two dbt models and tell me which one has more downstream tables.
cases.rationale: dbt model comparison -- wrong domain
